text: 49g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
10.7g of carbs. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for a seed, you want real nutrient concentration, not just tiny-food hype.
30.2g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
vitamin e at 15% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 141% daily value, zinc at 71% daily value, iron at 49% daily value stand out most. that is major mineral support.

on the plus side: often brings dense micronutrient support. that is part of what makes the category interesting. usually brings decent fat support. that is one of the category strengths. easy to sprinkle into meals. that practical versatility matters.

now the downsides: calories concentrate quickly. even useful support foods can get dense fast. calorie density still stacks up fast. tiny foods can still overshoot fast. texture or prep can be a barrier. not everyone uses seeds consistently.
B tier. Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
